LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA'S AND PERRY BOUSSINA'S MOTION TO COMPEL MEDIATION/ARBITRATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA ("BOUSSINAS") HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THEY WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT CONTAINING THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. GENERALLY, "SOMEONE NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT CANNOT BE COMPELLED TO ARBITRATE." (SKY SPORTS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2011) 201 CAL.APP.4TH 1363, 1367.) FURTHER, THE CAUSES OF ACTION AT ISSUE ARISE OUT OF THE WORK PERFORMED ON THE BAR BY PLAINTIFF WHICH HE CONTENDS UNJUSTLY ENRICHED THE BOUSINNAS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Notice Of Motion And Motion In Law & Equity To: 1. Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Default Entered In Violation Of Ccp 585(D); Procured By Fraud; 2. Order To Vacate Order Of Judge Pro Tem Paul A. Renne As In Excess Of The Court'S Jurisdiction; 3. Order Vacating The Judgment By Stipulation
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN L. PIPER, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-6286 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN MILLER's Motion In Law & Equity To: 1. Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Default Entered In Violation Of Ccp 585(D); Procured By Fraud; 2. Order To Vacate Order Of Judge Pro Tem Paul A. Renne As In Excess Of The Court'S Jurisdiction; 3. Order Vacating The Judgment By Stipulation is denied.. Whether the default was void or properly entered is irrelevant/moot. The parties subsequently entered into a negotiated settlement/stipulation to resolve the case. Taintor v. Sup. Crt. (1949) 95 Cal.App.2d 346 does not preclude parties from settling a case after a default and there is a strong policy in favor of encouraging settlement. There is no basis to vacate the discovery order and plaintiff properly cites SF local rule 10B. Defendant stipulated to the discovery Judge Pro tem. Moreover, the enforceability of the discovery order is irrelevant/moot based on the settlement. Defendant fails to demonstrate "duress" to vacate the stipulation/settlement. The duress was self-created by the failure to comply with discovery mandates. In addition, there was nothing stopping defendant from seeking relief from the discovery sanctions. There is no authority holding that a settlement must be global to be enforceable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JONATHAN PIPER, 415.362.6286, FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN STEVENS.
JAMES DOMBROSKI, ESQ., 707.762.7807, AND JEFFREY PERKINS, ESQ., 415.302.1115, FOR DEFENDANTS PERRY BOUSSINA AND TOURAJ BOUSSINA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Cross Complainants Touraj Boussina's and Perry Boussina's Motion To Compel Mediation/Arbitration IS DEnied. Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina ("Boussinas") have not established that they were not parties to the subscription agreement containing the arbitration clause. Generally, "someone not a party to the arbitration agreement cannot be compelled to arbitrate." (Sky Sports, Inc. v. Superior Court (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 1363, 1367.) Further, the causes of action at issue arise out of the work performed on the bar by Plaintiff which he contends unjustly enriched the Bousinnas. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
